title: Kirateshwar Mahadev Temple
tagline: Kirateshwar Mahadev Temple — spiritual town of Sikkim

overview.short: Kirateshwar Mahadev Temple (Nepali: किराँतेश्वर महादेव मन्दिर) is a Hindu temple, identified to be a Hindu pilgrimage site which is located at Legship, Gyalshing district, India al
overview.description: Kirateshwar Mahadev Temple (Nepali: किराँतेश्वर महादेव मन्दिर) is a Hindu temple, identified to be a Hindu pilgrimage site which is located at Legship, Gyalshing district, India along the banks of River Rangeet, which has many episodes of the Mahabharata attached to it. The temple is also known as Kirateshwar Mahadev Thaan by the Kirati people or simply known as Shiv Mandir.

topPlaces.name: Hee Gyathang Monastery
topPlaces.description: Hee Gyathang Monastery is a Buddhist monastery situated in Upper Dzongu, North Sikkim in northeastern India. It was built by hermit Abi Putso Rangdrol in 1914.

topPlaces.name: Upper Dzongu Forest Block
topPlaces.description: Upper Dzongu Forest Block is a village in Mangan subdivision, North Sikkim district, Sikkim, India. The Ministry of Home Affairs has given it a geographical code of 260908.

topPlaces.name: Gor, Sikkim
topPlaces.description: Gor is a village in Mangan subdivision, North Sikkim district, Sikkim, India. The Ministry of Home Affairs has given it a geographical code of 260882.

topPlaces.name: Gnon-Samdong
topPlaces.description: Gnon-Sangdong is a small village under Hee-Gyathang GPU in Dzongu subdivision, Mangan, Sikkim, India. The Ministry of Home Affairs has given it a geographical code of 260883.

topPlaces.name: Hee-Gyathang
topPlaces.description: Hee-Gyathang is a village in Mangan subdivision, North Sikkim district, Sikkim, India. The Ministry of Home Affairs has given it a geographical code of 260884.

topPlaces.name: Sangtok
topPlaces.description: Sangtok is a village in Mangan subdivision, North Sikkim district, Sikkim, India. The Ministry of Home Affairs has given it a geographical code of 260881.

topPlaces.name: Dikchu
topPlaces.description: Dikchu is a small town in the Gangtok District of the Indian state of Sikkim. The town lies at the confluence of the Dikchu River with the Teesta River and is the site of the 96 MW Dikchu River Hydroelectric Power Project as well as 510 MW Teesta V Hydroelectric Power Project.

topPlaces.name: Barfok
topPlaces.description: Barfok is a village in Mangan subdivision, North Sikkim district, Sikkim, India. The Ministry of Home Affairs has given it a geographical code of 260885.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Kirateshwar Mahadev Temple?
faq.a: The best time to visit Kirateshwar Mahadev Temple is Mar – Jun, Sep – Nov. Summers are 15–28°C and winters are 2–16°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Kirateshwar Mahadev Temple?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Pakyong Airport, Gangtok (28 km) and the nearest railway station is Siliguri Junction (82 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Kirateshwar Mahadev Temple?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Kirateshwar Mahadev Temple at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Kirateshwar Mahadev Temple?
faq.a: Stays in Kirateshwar Mahadev Temple start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Kirateshwar Mahadev Temple?
faq.a: The top attractions are Hee Gyathang Monastery, Upper Dzongu Forest Block, Gor, Sikkim, Gnon-Samdong, Hee-Gyathang.
